meta.title: 500 AI Agents Atlas
meta.description: A React atlas for 500+ AI agent projects, runnable implementations, use cases, and the CrewAI MCP course.

home.eyebrow: Course front page plus project atlas
home.title: 500+ AI Agent Projects
home.description: A React atlas for learning, browsing, running, and redirecting into the strongest AI agent examples in this repository.
home.courseTitle: CrewAI with FastMCP-style lessons at the front
home.courseDescription: The course is the guided entry point, then the atlas opens into local code agents and README-linked external examples.
home.searchTitle: Search every local agent and README table row

search.placeholder: Search agents, use cases, frameworks, industries

course.title: CrewAI with FastMCP-style integration
course.fallbackDescription: A practical course for building CrewAI workflows with MCP-style tool boundaries.
course.learningDescription: Each lesson has its own page with objectives, run commands, code files, and a code walkthrough surface.

agents.title: Local code pages for every implementation
agents.description: Each agent page explains the framework, setup, run commands, architecture, and code structure from the files in agents/.
agents.defaultWorkflow: Creates a {framework} workflow around the core task
agents.defaultInput: Accepts user input through the command line or defaults
agents.defaultTools: Calls tools, models, or local data helpers
agents.defaultResult: Returns a structured result for the user

useCases.title: Dedicated page for every README use case
useCases.description: The catalog is parsed from the repository README tables, so each external project gets a page, source section, and redirect route.
useCases.whatThisAddsDescription: This page turns a README table row into a durable catalog entry with context, framework grouping, industry filtering, and a redirect route for the source project.

frameworks.description: Compare the main framework families represented in this repository, then jump into matching agents and examples.

code.noImports: No import block in this file section.
code.lineCount: {count} non-empty lines are bundled into this page.

notFound.title: This atlas page was not found
notFound.description: The catalog route does not match a local agent, lesson, use case, or framework.

lesson-01.title: First CrewAI Researcher
lesson-01.summary: Build a single researcher agent, give it a clear role, run one task, and inspect the response path.
objectives: Attach the agent to one clear task
Run the crew and read the final output

lesson-02.title: Multi-Agent Research Crew
lesson-02.summary: Split research, writing, and editing responsibilities into a sequential CrewAI workflow.
objectives: Connect tasks into a sequential process
Pass context from researcher to writer to editor
Understand where tools fit into CrewAI

lesson-03.title: CrewAI With FastMCP-Style Tools
lesson-03.summary: Use a FastMCP-style tool surface to let CrewAI agents query structured project data and produce a status report.
objectives: Model a tool contract that resembles MCP server access
Let agents request project records through tools
Generate a management-ready project status report
See how MCP-style boundaries improve agent design

langgraph.bestFor: Stateful workflows, graph control, retries, RAG, and production routing.
langgraph.decision: Choose it when the agent needs explicit state transitions and reliable recovery paths.

crewai.bestFor: Role-based teams, business automation, repeatable task handoffs, and fast prototypes.
crewai.decision: Choose it when the project maps naturally to specialists with goals and tasks.

autogen.bestFor: Code generation, research collaboration, feedback loops, and executable experiments.
autogen.decision: Choose it when agents need to talk, critique, run code, and iterate.

agno.bestFor: Lightweight tool agents, quick iteration, model flexibility, and compact apps.
agno.decision: Choose it when you want a focused tool-calling agent with low ceremony.

llamaindex.bestFor: Document Q&A, enterprise retrieval, data connectors, and knowledge workflows.
llamaindex.decision: Choose it when the data pipeline matters as much as the agent behavior.
